prompts.text: {player1}, welcome to the bar! Take 2 sips to kick things off.
{player1}, you're buying the first round of sips. Give out 3 however you want.
{player1}, ask the bartender what their favorite drink to make is. Take 2 sips while they answer.
{player1}, give out 2 sips to whoever tips the least. Group decides.
{player1}, cheers to the bartender! Take 2 sips.
{player1}, give out 2 sips to whoever took the longest to decide what to order.
First to shout it out: What are the 3 ingredients in a classic Margarita? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Tequila, lime juice, and triple sec (or Cointreau). Served with salt on the rim.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What cocktail is made with vodka, coffee liqueur, and espresso? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Espresso Martini -- invented in the 1980s by London bartender Dick Bradsell when a model asked for something to 'wake me up and f*** me up'

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What are the 2 main ingredients in a Moscow Mule? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Vodka and ginger beer (with lime). It's traditionally served in a copper mug.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What cocktail was supposedly invented at Harry's Bar in Venice? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Bellini -- prosecco and white peach puree, created by Giuseppe Cipriani around 1948

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the base spirit in a Mojito? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: White rum -- mixed with sugar, lime juice, soda water, and muddled mint. It originated in Havana, Cuba.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What cocktail is made with bourbon, sugar, bitters, and an orange peel? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Old Fashioned -- one of the oldest known cocktails, dating back to the early 1800s

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What are the ingredients in a Long Island Iced Tea? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Vodka, tequila, rum, gin, triple sec, sour mix, and a splash of cola. Despite having no actual tea, it looks and somewhat tastes like iced tea.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What cocktail is traditionally made with gin, vermouth, and an olive or lemon twist? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The Martini -- James Bond famously orders his 'shaken, not stirred' (though purists say stirring is correct for a clear drink)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What cocktail is made with whiskey, sweet vermouth, and bitters? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A Manhattan -- created in the 1870s, supposedly at the Manhattan Club in New York City

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is a Negroni made of? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Equal parts gin, Campari, and sweet vermouth. Created in Florence, Italy around 1919 when Count Negroni asked for his Americano with gin instead of soda.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What cocktail uses rum, lime juice, and sugar, shaken with ice? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The Daiquiri -- originally from Cuba. Hemingway's favorite bar in Havana, El Floridita, serves a frozen version named after him.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the base spirit in a Cosmopolitan? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Vodka -- mixed with triple sec, cranberry juice, and fresh lime juice. Made famous by Sex and the City.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What cocktail was created for the Kentucky Derby? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Mint Julep -- bourbon, sugar, water, and mint. Over 120,000 are served at Churchill Downs during the Derby each year.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What's in a Pina Colada? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Rum, coconut cream, and pineapple juice -- it became Puerto Rico's official national drink in 1978

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What cocktail is made with tequila, orange juice, and grenadine? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Tequila Sunrise -- named for its appearance. The grenadine sinks and creates a gradient that looks like a sunrise.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country does tequila come from, and what is it made from? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Mexico -- made from the blue agave plant. By law, true tequila can only be produced in specific regions of Mexico.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the difference between bourbon and whiskey? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: All bourbon is whiskey, but not all whiskey is bourbon. Bourbon must be made in the US, from at least 51% corn, aged in new charred oak barrels.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What grain is vodka traditionally made from? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Traditionally potatoes or wheat/rye -- but legally, vodka can be made from any fermentable material. Some use grapes, corn, or even milk.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What makes Scotch different from other whiskies? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Scotch must be made in Scotland, aged at least 3 years in oak barrels, and made from malted barley. The spelling 'whisky' (no 'e') is the Scottish convention.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is absinthe traditionally nicknamed? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Green Fairy -- it was banned in many countries for decades because of claims it caused hallucinations (mostly due to the chemical thujone, though the danger was exaggerated)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What Japanese spirit is made from sweet potatoes, barley, or rice? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Shochu -- Japan's most popular spirit by volume, more popular than sake. Not to be confused with soju (Korean).

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What spirit is made from sugarcane juice or molasses? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Rum -- it originated in the Caribbean in the 17th century. Pirates really did drink a lot of rum.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is mezcal, and how is it different from tequila? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Mezcal is made from any type of agave (over 30 varieties), while tequila can only use blue agave. Mezcal is often smokier because the agave is roasted in underground pits.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the worm in a tequila bottle actually? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: It's actually a moth larva (gusano), and it's found in some mezcal bottles -- not tequila. It was originally a marketing gimmick from the 1950s.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country produces the most wine in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Italy -- followed closely by France and Spain. Together they produce about half the world's wine.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What makes champagne actually Champagne? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: It must come from the Champagne region of France and be made using the methode champenoise. Everything else is just 'sparkling wine.'

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does 'proof' mean on a liquor bottle? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: In the US, proof is double the alcohol percentage. So 80 proof = 40% ABV. The term comes from the old practice of 'proving' alcohol by soaking gunpowder in it -- if it still ignited, it was 'proof.'

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the strongest commercially available spirit? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: Spirytus Rektyfikowany from Poland -- 96% ABV (192 proof). It's essentially pure grain alcohol.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does the word 'cocktail' originally mean? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The earliest known definition (1806) described it as 'a stimulating liquor composed of spirits, sugar, water, and bitters.' The origin of the word itself is debated -- one theory involves a mixed-breed horse with a cocked tail.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is a speakeasy? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: An illicit bar during Prohibition (1920-1933) in the US. The name comes from 'speaking easy' -- keeping your voice down so police wouldn't hear.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How long did Prohibition last in the United States? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 13 years -- from 1920 to 1933 (18th Amendment to 21st Amendment). Alcohol consumption actually increased during Prohibition.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the oldest bar in the United States? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The White Horse Tavern in Newport, Rhode Island -- established in 1673. It's been operating for over 350 years.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country has the highest alcohol consumption per capita? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Czech Republic -- largely due to beer. Czechs drink about 40 gallons of beer per person per year.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is 'last call' and why does it exist? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The final opportunity to order drinks before a bar closes. It exists due to liquor laws that set mandatory closing times -- varies by state/country.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does 'on the rocks' mean? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Served over ice -- the 'rocks' are ice cubes. 'Neat' means no ice, room temperature. 'Up' means shaken/stirred with ice then strained.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is a 'well drink'? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: A drink made with the bar's cheapest house liquor (the bottles kept in the 'well' or 'speed rail' at the bartender's station), as opposed to 'top shelf' or 'call' drinks.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the most ordered cocktail in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Margarita -- it's been the #1 most ordered cocktail globally for years running

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the original purpose of cocktail garnishes? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: To indicate what was in the drink -- before printed menus, bartenders would garnish drinks so servers could identify them. The olive in a martini, cherry in a Manhattan, etc.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the most popular beer in the world by sales? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Snow Beer -- a Chinese brand most people outside China have never heard of. It sells over 100 billion liters per year.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the difference between a lager and an ale? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The type of yeast and fermentation temperature. Lagers use bottom-fermenting yeast at cold temperatures. Ales use top-fermenting yeast at warmer temperatures. Ales are generally more flavorful.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country invented IPA (India Pale Ale)? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: England -- brewers added extra hops to preserve beer during the long sea voyage to British troops in India in the 1800s. The hops acted as a natural preservative.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the oldest known alcoholic beverage? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Mead -- fermented honey water. Evidence of mead production dates back to 7000 BC in China. It predates both beer and wine.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does ABV stand for on a beer label? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Alcohol By Volume -- the percentage of the drink that is pure alcohol. A typical beer is 4-6% ABV.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What gives beer its bitter flavor? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Hops -- the flowers of the hop plant (Humulus lupulus). They also act as a natural preservative and contribute to aroma.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country has the oldest beer purity law? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Germany -- the Reinheitsgebot (1516) stated beer could only be made from water, barley, and hops. Yeast was added later when its role was understood.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does 'dry' mean when describing wine? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: It means the wine has very little residual sugar -- the yeast consumed almost all the sugar during fermentation. It doesn't mean the wine literally dries your mouth (that's tannins).

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the most planted grape variety in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Cabernet Sauvignon -- grown in virtually every wine-producing country. It's a cross between Cabernet Franc and Sauvignon Blanc.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What color grape is used to make rose wine? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Red grapes -- rose gets its pink color from limited contact with red grape skins. It's not a mix of red and white wine (with rare exceptions like Champagne).

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Why do people swirl wine before drinking it? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: To aerate it -- swirling increases the surface area exposed to air, which releases aromatic compounds and opens up the flavors. It's not just for show.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the legal drinking age in most of Europe? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: 18 -- though some countries like Germany and Belgium allow beer and wine at 16

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In what country is it customary to look people in the eyes while toasting? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Germany (and much of Central Europe) -- in Germany, not making eye contact during a toast is said to bring 7 years of bad sex

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does 'hair of the dog' mean and where does it come from? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Drinking alcohol to cure a hangover. The full phrase is 'hair of the dog that bit you' -- from the medieval belief that putting hair from a rabid dog into a bite wound would cure rabies.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the most expensive bottle of wine ever sold? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A 1947 French Cheval Blanc -- sold for $304,375 in 2010. Though a 1945 Romanee-Conti sold for $558,000 in 2018.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many grapes does it take to make one bottle of wine? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 600-800 grapes -- roughly 2.5 pounds of grapes per bottle

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is a 'Black and Tan'? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A layered beer drink -- pale ale on the bottom and stout (usually Guinness) on top. The name is controversial in Ireland due to its association with a British paramilitary group.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Why is the bottom of a wine bottle indented? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: It's called a 'punt' -- it strengthens the bottle, collects sediment, and makes it easier to pour. Deeper punts were historically associated with higher quality (and higher price).

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the 'angel's share'? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The portion of whiskey or wine that evaporates from the barrel during aging -- typically 2-4% per year. Over decades, a significant amount is lost to the 'angels.'

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is a sommelier? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: A trained wine professional -- they specialize in wine service, food pairing, and wine selection. Master Sommelier is one of the hardest certifications in the world (fewer than 300 people have passed).

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is 'muddling' in bartending? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Pressing/crushing ingredients (like mint, fruit, or sugar) in the bottom of a glass to release their flavors and oils. Essential for Mojitos and Old Fashioneds.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is a 'jigger' in bartending? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: A measuring tool used to pour precise amounts of liquid -- typically 1.5 oz on one side and 0.75 oz on the other

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does it mean to 'build' a drink? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: To make the drink directly in the serving glass (no shaker needed) -- like a gin and tonic or rum and coke

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Why do bartenders shake some drinks and stir others? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Shaking is for drinks with citrus, cream, or egg -- it aerates and chills quickly. Stirring is for spirit-forward drinks (Martini, Manhattan) to keep them clear and silky.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is 'free pouring'? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Pouring liquor without a jigger, using counting and technique to measure. An experienced bartender counts seconds (usually 3-4 count for a standard pour of 1.5 oz).

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is a 'back' when ordering at a bar? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: A non-alcoholic drink served alongside your main drink -- like ordering 'whiskey with a water back' or 'tequila with a pickle back'

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What actually causes a hangover? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Multiple factors: dehydration, inflammation, acetaldehyde toxicity (a byproduct of alcohol metabolism), electrolyte imbalance, and disrupted sleep. There's no single cause.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Does mixing different types of alcohol make your hangover worse? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Not directly -- the total amount of alcohol matters more. But mixing often means you drink more without realizing it. The saying 'beer before liquor' has no scientific basis.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How long does it take the average person to metabolize one standard drink? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 1 hour -- your liver processes roughly one standard drink (1.5 oz liquor, 5 oz wine, 12 oz beer) per hour. Nothing speeds this up -- not coffee, cold showers, or food.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What congeners are and why dark liquors cause worse hangovers? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Congeners are chemical byproducts of fermentation/aging. Dark spirits (bourbon, red wine, brandy) have more congeners than clear spirits (vodka, gin), which contributes to worse hangovers.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: At what blood alcohol content (BAC) are you legally drunk in most US states? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: 0.08% -- though impairment starts at much lower levels. Utah is the strictest at 0.05%.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the national drink of Japan? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Sake -- technically a rice wine, though the brewing process is more similar to beer. The best sake is served slightly chilled, not warm.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What drink do you traditionally toast with in Russia? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Vodka -- and you're supposed to drink the whole shot, not sip it. Toasting with an empty glass is considered bad luck.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the world's oldest known recipe? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A recipe for beer -- found on a Sumerian clay tablet from around 3900 BC. It's essentially a hymn to the goddess of beer, Ninkasi.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What popular cocktail was invented by a pharmacist? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Coca-Cola -- John Pemberton, a pharmacist, created it in 1886. The original recipe included coca leaf extract (cocaine) and kola nuts (caffeine).

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What island nation has the highest per-capita rum consumption? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: India is actually the world's largest rum consumer by volume, but per capita it's likely the Caribbean island nations like Jamaica or Barbados

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is a 'boilermaker'? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A beer paired with a shot of whiskey -- you can either drink them separately or drop the shot glass into the beer (called a 'depth charge')

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What cocktail did Hemingway famously drink at the Ritz in Paris? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Dry Martini -- he reportedly said 'I like to drink a Martini, two at the very most. After three I'm under the table, after four I'm under my host.'

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is 'cask strength' whiskey? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Whiskey bottled straight from the barrel without adding water to reduce the proof. It's typically 50-65% ABV compared to the standard 40%.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the standard tip for a bartender in the US? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: $1-2 per drink or 15-20% of the tab. Tipping well gets you faster service, stronger pours, and the bartender actually remembering your name.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does it mean when a bartender 'comps' your drink? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: They give it to you for free -- usually as a gesture of goodwill for loyal customers, after a long wait, or to smooth over a mistake

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is 'top shelf' liquor? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The premium, most expensive spirits -- literally stored on the highest shelf behind the bar because they're grabbed less often than the cheaper 'well' liquor at arm level

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What animal has the highest alcohol tolerance? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The pen-tailed tree shrew of Malaysia -- it drinks fermented nectar equivalent to 10-12 glasses of wine per night and shows no signs of intoxication

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Can you actually die from a hangover? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Not from the hangover itself, but severe dehydration, alcohol poisoning, or withdrawal symptoms can be fatal. About 95,000 Americans die from alcohol-related causes annually.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the oldest brewery still in operation? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Weihenstephan Brewery in Germany -- founded in 1040 AD by Benedictine monks. That's nearly 1,000 years of continuous beer production.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many bubbles are in a glass of champagne? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 49 million -- scientists at the University of Reims actually counted (sort of). The bubbles are caused by CO2 nucleating on tiny imperfections in the glass.

prompts.text: {player1}, ask the bartender what the most popular drink tonight is. If you've had one, give out 3 sips. If not, take 3.
{player1}, ask the bartender to name 3 cocktails with gin. If they nail it (they will), take 2 sips in respect.
{player1}, challenge the bartender: What's in a Negroni? If they get it right, everyone takes 2 sips. If not, the bartender takes a bow.
{player1}, ask the bartender what their least favorite drink to make is. If it's what you ordered, take 4 sips.
{player1}, ask the bartender to recommend a drink for you based on your vibe. You MUST order it or take 5 sips.
{player1}, guess the bartender's age. Off by more than 3 years? Take 3 sips.
{player1}, ask the bartender what the weirdest order they've gotten tonight is. Take 2 sips for the privilege of that knowledge.
{player1}, ask the bartender if they prefer making cocktails or pouring beers. Take 2 sips whatever they say.
{player1}, ask the bartender to name 5 types of whiskey. If they can, everyone cheers and takes 2 sips.
{player1}, ask the bartender what they'd drink if they were sitting on your side. Take 2 sips while they think.
{player1}, compliment the bartender. If it's genuine (group judges), give out 3 sips. If it's weak, take 3.
{player1}, ask the bartender to make you their signature drink. You MUST drink it. If you love it, give out 3 sips. Hate it? Take 3 anyway.
{player1}, ask the bartender what their go-to shot is. Group must take it or everyone takes 3 sips.
{player1}, guess the brand of house vodka. Ask the bartender. Right = give out 4 sips. Wrong = take 3.
{player1}, ask the bartender what the best-tipping table tonight was. If they point at your group, everyone gives out 2. If not, shame -- everyone takes 2.
{player1}, ask the bartender to tell you one thing people do that annoys them. If you've done it tonight, take 4 sips.
{player1}, ask the bartender: 'What's in a Dark and Stormy?' If they know, take 2 sips in respect. If not, give out 2.
{player1}, order whatever the person next to you at the bar (not in your group) is drinking. Take 2 sips of courage first.
{player1}, ask the bartender how long they've been bartending. Take 1 sip per year (max 5).
{player1}, ask the bartender to name a cocktail most people order wrong. Take 2 sips for the education.
{player1}, ask the bartender what time it gets the craziest on a typical night. If it's right now, everyone takes 3 sips.
{player1}, tip the bartender $1 right now. If you don't have cash, take 4 sips.
{player1}, ask the bartender to settle this: Is a hot dog a sandwich? Take 2 sips regardless of the answer.
{player1}, ask the bartender what cocktail they think you'd order based on your look. If they're right, give out 4 sips. Wrong = take 3.
{player1}, ask the bartender if they've ever cut someone off tonight. Take 2 sips. Hope it's not you next.
{player1}, ask the bartender: What drink has the highest profit margin? Take 2 sips for insider knowledge.
{player1}, ask the bartender to give your group a rating from 1-10. Under 7? Everyone takes 3 sips.
{player1}, ask the bartender for a drink recommendation under $10. You have to order it. Take 2 sips while you wait.
{player1}, ask the bartender what their favorite shot to pour is. If anyone in your group has had it tonight, they give out 3 sips.
{player1}, compliment the bartender's speed. If they smile, give out 3 sips. Stone-faced? Take 3.
{player1}, ask the bartender: 'What's the most overrated cocktail?' Take 2 sips. If you ordered it tonight, take 4.
{player1}, ask the bartender if they can tell when someone is on a bad date. Take 2 sips for the gossip.
{player1}, challenge the bartender to name 5 tequila brands in 10 seconds. Take 2 sips win or lose.
{player1}, ask the bartender what the most expensive item on the menu is. Group splits the sips: take 1 sip per $10 of the price (max 5).
{player1}, ask the bartender if they prefer the early or late crowd. Take 2 sips.
{player1}, order a drink with no ice and see if the bartender gives you more liquor or just less drink. Report back. Take 2 sips.
{player1}, ask the bartender what cocktail they'd make if they could only use 3 ingredients. Take 2 sips for the wisdom.
{player1}, ask the bartender: 'What should I absolutely NOT order here?' Take 3 sips if you've already ordered it.
{player1}, tell the bartender it's your friend's birthday (point to {player2}). If the bartender does something nice, {player2} gives out 3 sips. If they see through the lie, {player1} takes 4.
{player1}, ask the bartender if they prefer making classic or modern cocktails. Take 2 sips and have a real conversation.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. The bar tab doesn't care about your feelings.
{player1}, take 3 sips if you've ever been cut off at a bar.
{player1}, take 2 sips for every cocktail you've had tonight.
{player1}, take 3 sips if you Googled 'best bars near me' before coming here.
{player1}, take 2 sips if you're drinking a beer right now. 3 if it's a light beer.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. The bartender's watching.
{player1}, take 3 sips if you've ever ordered a drink just because it sounded cool.
{player1}, take 2 sips if you can name the brand of beer on tap. Can't? Take 4.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. Open tabs are dangerous and so is this game.
{player1}, take 3 sips if you've ever asked 'what's good here?' at a bar.
{player1}, take 2 sips if your drink has a straw in it right now.
{player1}, take 3 sips if you've been to this bar before.
{player1}, take 2 sips for every hour you've been here already.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. The tip jar is watching.
{player1}, take 3 sips if you've ever left a bar without closing your tab. Shame.
{player1}, take 2 sips if you ordered the same thing as someone else tonight.
{player1}, take 3 sips if you've ever pretended to like a drink to seem cool.
{player1}, take 2 sips if you know the bartender's name. Don't? Take 3.
{player1}, take 3 sips if you've ever been the last one standing at bar close.
{player1}, take 2 sips if you drove here. Actually, switch to water after this game.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. The jukebox demands it.
{player1}, take 3 sips if you've ever been to a karaoke bar.
{player1}, take 2 sips if your phone battery is under 30%. You'll need it for the Uber.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. The bar stool chose you.
{player1}, take 3 sips if you've ever tipped less than 15%. Repent.
{player1}, take 2 sips if you've ever closed down a bar on a weeknight.
{player1}, take 3 sips if you ordered something off-menu tonight.
{player1}, take 3 sips if you've been to a dive bar this month.
{player1}, take 2 sips if you'd rather be at a rooftop bar right now.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. The happy hour gods demand tribute.
{player1}, give out {sip_count} sips to whoever ordered the weakest drink tonight.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever has been to the fewest bars in their life.
{player1}, give out 2 sips to whoever would get cut off first.
{player1}, give {sip_count} sips to whoever tips the worst. Group decides.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever is nursing their drink the slowest.
{player1}, give out 2 sips to whoever ordered the most basic drink tonight.
{player1}, give {sip_count} sips to whoever is the loudest at the bar right now.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever you'd want as your bartender wingman.
{player1}, give out 2 sips to whoever is most likely to start a tab and forget to close it.
{player1}, give {sip_count} sips to whoever ordered last.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever has the highest bar tab right now (estimate).
{player1}, give out 2 sips to whoever has the worst taste in drinks.
{player1}, give {sip_count} sips to whoever is the biggest beer snob.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever would make the worst bartender.
{player1}, give out 2 sips to whoever looks like they need another round.
{player1}, give {sip_count} sips to whoever showed up latest tonight.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever knows the most about wine. Knowledge is punishment.
{player1}, give out 2 sips to whoever has the most embarrassing drunk story from a bar.
{player1}, give {sip_count} sips to whoever orders the same thing every time they go out.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever would get the bartender's number (or thinks they could).
{player1}, give out 2 sips to whoever drinks the slowest.
{player1}, give {sip_count} sips to whoever has been the most sober this whole time.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever would win a drinking contest here.
{player1}, give out 2 sips to whoever is already thinking about tomorrow's hangover.
{player1}, give {sip_count} sips to whoever you'd trust to order you a drink blind.
Everyone hold up how many drinks you've had tonight. Lowest takes 3 sips. Catch up.
Everyone hold up how many different bars you've been to this month. Lowest takes 3 sips.
Everyone name a cocktail. Last person drinks 3 sips.
Everyone name a beer brand. Last person drinks 3 sips.
Everyone hold up how many countries you've had a drink in. Lowest takes 3 sips.
Everyone name a type of whiskey. Last person drinks 3 sips.
Everyone hold up how many bartenders you know by name. Lowest takes 3 sips.
Everyone name a type of wine. Last person drinks 3 sips.
Everyone hold up how much you've tipped tonight (round to the nearest dollar). Lowest takes 3 sips.
Everyone name a shot. Last person takes 3 sips.
Everyone who has been kicked out of a bar -- take 3 sips.
Everyone who has ever bartended (even for a house party) -- give out 2 sips.
Everyone who ordered a beer tonight -- take 2 sips.
Everyone who has a go-to bar order -- take 1 sip. Don't have one? Take 3.
Everyone who has ever done a pub crawl -- take 2 sips.
Everyone who has left their card at a bar before -- take 3 sips.
Everyone who has sent a drunk text from this bar tonight -- take 3 sips.
Everyone who has ever blacked out at a bar -- take 2 sips. No details needed.
Everyone who has ever been the designated driver -- give out 3 sips. Heroes.
Everyone who ordered a cocktail tonight -- take 2 sips.
Everyone who has a favorite bartender anywhere -- give out 2 sips.
Everyone who has vomited in or near a bar -- take 3 sips. We're not judging. (We are.)
Everyone who knows how to make at least 3 cocktails from scratch -- give out 2 sips.
Everyone who has ever dined and dashed (any restaurant/bar) -- take 4 sips.
Everyone who has asked 'what do you recommend?' tonight -- take 2 sips.
Everyone take 2 sips. We're at a bar. It's what we do.
Everyone who has had more than 3 drinks tonight -- take 2 sips. It's adding up.
Everyone who has been to a speakeasy -- give out 2 sips.
Everyone who has ever fallen off a barstool -- take 3 sips.
Everyone who has ever started a tab and immediately regretted it -- take 2 sips.
Everyone who is drinking something clear right now -- take 2 sips.
Everyone who has been to a bar in another country -- give out 2 sips.
Everyone who has a 'bar story' they're embarrassed about -- take 3 sips.
Everyone who has ever karaoked at a bar -- take 2 sips.
Everyone who has bought a stranger a drink -- give out 2 sips.
Everyone who would trust the bartender to pick their next drink -- take 2 sips.
Everyone who has been here for more than 2 hours -- take 3 sips. Time flies.
Everyone who is drinking on a budget tonight -- take 2 sips. Water is free.
Everyone who has made a friend at a bar -- give out 2 sips.
Everyone who has ever been overcharged at a bar and said nothing -- take 3 sips.
Everyone who has tried to impress someone with their drink order -- take 2 sips.
Everyone who has a drink in their hand right now -- take 2 sips. If not, what are you doing?
Everyone who tipped over 20% tonight -- give out 2 sips.
Everyone who has taken a shot tonight already -- take 2 more sips.
Everyone who has been to a rooftop bar -- give out 1 sip.
Everyone who has bartender friends -- give out 2 sips. Hook up the group.
Everyone who has closed out a bar on a weeknight -- take 3 sips. Wild behavior.
Everyone who has ever judged someone's drink order -- take 2 sips. Be honest.
Everyone wearing something you wouldn't wear to a dive bar -- take 2 sips.
Everyone who has ever played a bar game (pool, darts, etc.) tonight -- give out 2 sips.
On 3, point to who would be the best bartender. They give out 3 sips.
On 3, point to who would get cut off first tonight. Most pointed-at takes 3 sips.

{player1} vs {player2}: Name cocktails back and forth. First to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name beer brands. Go back and forth. First to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name types of whiskey/bourbon. First to fail takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Who can name more ingredients in a Long Island Iced Tea? Loser takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name wine regions. Go back and forth. First to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Staring contest. First to blink drinks 3 sips. Bar rules.
{player1} vs {player2}: Rock paper scissors. Best of 3. Loser buys the winner their next drink... or takes 4 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name bars you've been to. Go back and forth. First to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name cocktail ingredients. Go back and forth (no repeats). First to fail takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Who has the higher bar tab right now? Lower tab takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name famous drinking songs. First to fail takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Who can finish their current sip faster? Loser takes 3 more sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name countries known for wine. First to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Thumb war. Loser takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name types of shots. Go back and forth. First to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Who tips better? Group votes. Cheapskate takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name happy hour specials from bars you know. First to fail takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name spirits (vodka, rum, gin, etc. -- specific brands). First to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Who can hold a straight face longer while the group tries to make them laugh? Loser takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name famous bars from movies or TV. First to fail takes 3 sips.
{player1} is the Barback. For the next {rule_turns} rounds, they must take a sip every time anyone else drinks. Solidarity.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1} is no longer the Barback. Freedom.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, no one can say 'drink,' 'shot,' or 'cheers.' Say it = 2 sips.
{player1} makes a bar rule. Everyone must follow it for {rule_turns} rounds. Break it = 2 sips.
For the next {rule_turns} rounds, you must toast someone specific before every sip. No toast = 2 extra sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Toasting rule is over. Drink in silence.

prompts.text: Thumbmaster! {player1} is the Thumbmaster. When they put their thumb on the bar, last to notice takes 2 sips. Lasts {rule_turns} rounds.
For the next {rule_turns} rounds, left hand only. Use your right? Take 2 sips.
For the next {rule_turns} rounds, no phones. Get caught = 3 sips. You're at a bar with friends.
prompts.rule_end_text: Phone ban is lifted. Doom scroll away.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, pinky out when you drink. Forget = 2 sips. Stay classy.
For the next {rule_turns} rounds, before taking a sip, you must name a cocktail you haven't named yet. Can't = double sips.
{player1} picks a drinking buddy. For {rule_turns} rounds, whenever one drinks, both drink.
prompts.rule_end_text: The drinking buddy pact is over.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, you must whisper. Normal volume at the bar = 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Whispering at the bar is over. Be loud.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, no one can say anyone's name. Bar nicknames only. Slip up = 2 sips.
For the next {rule_turns} rounds, last person to raise their glass when someone says 'CHEERS' takes 2 sips.
{player1} is the Question Master. For {rule_turns} rounds, if they ask you a question and you answer, you drink 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1} is no longer the Question Master.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, you must look at the bartender before taking each sip. Forget = 2 sips.
For the next {rule_turns} rounds, no cursing at the bar. Every curse = 1 sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: Cursing is back on the menu.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, you must say 'bottoms up' before every sip. Forget = 2 extra sips.
For the next {rule_turns} rounds, no one can order the same drink as someone else. If you do, take 3 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Copy-cat rule is over. Order what you want.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, last person to tap the bar when someone says 'LAST CALL' takes 2 sips.
For the next {rule_turns} rounds, anyone who finishes their drink must buy the next person a shot... or take 4 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Buy-a-shot rule is over. Wallets are safe.

prompts.text: {player1}, take {sip_count} sips. The tab grows stronger.
{player1}, take a shot. Can't? Take 4 sips instead. The bar demands it.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. No questions asked. Open tab energy.
{player1}, take 5 sips. The bartender poured this round heavy.
{player1}, take a shot. Can't? Take 5 sips. Last call is coming.
{player1}, take 5 sips. Consider it a cover charge.
{player1}, take a shot or 4 sips. The bar always wins.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. Your tab just went up.
{player1}, take 5 sips. The jukebox plays for blood.
{player1}, take a shot. Can't? Take 4 sips. The bartender is watching.
{player1}, take a shot or 4 sips. For the culture of bar games.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. The neon sign said 'drink.'
Heaven! Last person to raise their glass drinks 3 sips. Bar edition.
Waterfall! Everyone starts drinking. You can only stop when the person to your right stops. {player1} starts.
Bar crawl within the bar! Everyone must move to a different seat. Last to sit takes 3 sips.
Shot roulette! Everyone puts a finger on the table. {player1} counts down from 3. On 'go,' pull your finger or leave it. Majority rules -- minority takes 3 sips.
Cheers to the bartender! Everyone raise your glass to the bartender. Last to raise takes 3 sips.
Jukebox roulette! {player1} plays a random song. If the group hates it, {player1} takes 4 sips. If it bangs, give out 4.
Everyone order a drink for someone else at the table. You MUST drink what's ordered for you. Worst order (group votes) takes 3 extra sips.
Bar dare! {player1}, talk to a stranger at the bar and get their name. Report back in 30 seconds. Fail = 4 sips.
Speed round! Go around the table. Everyone takes 1 sip as fast as possible. Last person takes 3 more.
Tab check! Everyone guess the total bar tab. Closest gives out 4 sips. Farthest takes 4.
Ask the bartender to pick who at this table looks like they need another drink the most. That person takes 3 sips.
Ask the bartender who at this table looks like they tip the best. Everyone else takes 2 sips.
Ask the bartender to rate {player1}'s drink order on a scale of 1–10. Under 5 — {player1} takes 3 sips. Over 5 — they give out 3.
Ask the bartender who here looks like a regular. That person takes 2 sips of honor.
Ask the bartender to pick the biggest lightweight at the table. That person takes 3 sips to prove them wrong.
Challenge the bartender: can they name 5 types of whiskey in 10 seconds? If they can, everyone takes 2 sips of respect. If not, {player1} gives out 3.
Ask the bartender to make up a cocktail name for {player1} based on their vibe. {player1} takes 2 sips no matter what they say.
Challenge the bartender to guess {player1}'s usual drink order. Right? {player1} takes 3. Wrong? {player1} gives out 3.
Ask the bartender for their craziest customer story. If they tell one, everyone takes 2 sips of respect. Too busy? {player1} takes 3.
Ask the bartender what the weirdest drink order tonight was. Everyone takes 1 sip while they answer.
Ask the bartender their least favorite drink to make. Anyone at the table who has ordered it tonight takes 3 sips of shame.
The bartender picks {player1}'s next drink. Whatever they recommend, {player1} orders it — and takes 2 sips of courage.
Ask the bartender to pick a number between 1 and 5. Everyone takes that many sips. The bartender controls your fate now.
Everyone guess the most popular drink at this bar. Ask the bartender. Everyone wrong takes 2 sips.
Everyone guess how long the bartender has worked here. Closest wins and gives out 3 sips.
